sources.note: Contributed the AI-tell catalog. Filtered from 37 patterns to the ~21 that fire in a technical register and compressed to one-line rows.
Contributed the composition rules. Strunk's 18 rules condensed to the 8 that apply to short technical prose. The elements-of-style reference files were dropped.

modifications: Authoring-first, not editing-first: humanizer's draft/audit/final rewrite loop, travel-blog example, DETECTION GUIDANCE, and PERSONALITY AND SOUL sections were dropped.
Scope narrowed to repo and forge prose. Voiced and long-form prose stays with humanizer.
Dropped curly-quote pattern (Claude emits straight ASCII) and the Wikipedia-article framing of the significance, notability, and challenges patterns.

Added a content rule (carry why, not what), length-and-silence rules, git-shaped worked examples, guidance for error messages, log lines, and code comments, and anti-patterns specific to agent-written git prose. None of these exist upstream.
Inverted upstream's diff-anchored-writing rule for commits, changelogs, and migration guides, where narrating the change is correct.
DO NOT TOUCH condensed to five lines and extended to identifiers, CLI flags, and file paths.
